author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
Death Passed Upon All by Sin.
title: Death Passed Upon All by Sin.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: But on account of what does the same apostle say, that we are reconciled to God by Christ, except on account of what we had become enemies?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: And what is this but sin?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Whence also the prophet says, “Your sins separate between you and God.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: On account of this separation, therefore, the Mediator was sent, that He might take away the sin of the world, by which we were separated as enemies, and that we, being reconciled, might be made from enemies children.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: About this, certainly, the apostle was speaking; hence it happened that he interposed what he says, “That sin entered by one man.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For these are his former words.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: He says, “But God commendeth His love towards us in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Much more, then, being now justified in His blood, we shall be saved from wrath through Him.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For if, when we were enemies, we were reconciled to God by the death of His Son, much more, being reconciled, we shall be saved in His life.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: And not only so, but glorying also in God through Jesus Christ our Lord, by whom also we have now received reconciliation.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Then he subjoins, “Therefore, as by one man sin entered into this world, and death by sin, and so passed upon all men, for in him all have sinned.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Why do the Pelagians evade this matter?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: If reconciliation through Christ is necessary to all men, on all men has passed sin by which we have become enemies, in order that we should have need of reconciliation.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: This reconciliation is in the laver of regeneration and in the flesh and blood of Christ, without which not even infants can have life in themselves.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For as there was one man for death on account of sin, so there is one man for life on account of righteousness; because “as in Adam all die, so also in Christ shall all be made alive;” and “as by the sin of one upon all men to condemnation, so also by the righteousness of one upon all men unto justification of life.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Who is there that has turned a deaf ear to these apostolical words with such hardiness of wicked impiety, as, having heard them, to contend that death passed upon us through Adam without sin, unless, indeed, they are opposers of the grace of God and enemies of the cross of Christ?—whose end is destruction if they continue in this obstinacy.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: But let it suffice to have said thus much for the sake of that serpentine subtlety of theirs, by which they wish to corrupt simple minds, and to turn them away from the simplicity of the faith, as if by the praise of the creature.
